entries.title: A string of high-profile scandals has put a spotlight on the secretive House Ethics Committee
In an interview, Carlson says Trump’s war in Iran shows the president has been captured by neoconservatives
As Barack Obama backs efforts he once opposed, Democrats are successfully countering President Trump’s redistricting push

The Trump administration has lobbied against legislation to regulate artificial intelligence in at least six Republican-led states, deepening a split in the party over the White House’s industry-friendly AI agenda
The Justice Department said it would end its criminal investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the obstacle that has stalled Kevin Warsh’s confirmation as his successor
The Justice Department will end its Powell probe, federal firing-squad executions are back, and startup cowboys mosey on over to Texas. Read more in today’s What’s News newsletter:
The Trump administration announced criminal charges, sanctions and multimillion-dollar rewards aimed at combating online scams coming out of Southeast Asia and targeting Americans
First ​psychedelics​, then ​pot​: ​inside the White House’s ​legalization ​binge​. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:
The Trump administration is reclassifying marijuana as a less dangerous drug, a policy shift that could make it much easier to buy and sell pot and reward investors in the cannabis industry
There’s still one thing that unites us: Congress. Americans across the board seem to agree—​the​y loathe that branch of government. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:
Republicans are increasingly worried that a battle President Trump started last summer to redraw congressional district lines has backfired and may hand more seats to Democrats
The main outside group supporting Democrats’ efforts to flip the House is reserving $272 million in TV and digital advertising ahead of the midterms, mostly targeting seats held by Republicans
Virginia voters approved a measure intended to boost the number of Democratic seats in the U.S. House by redrawing the state’s congressional map ahead of the midterms, the Associated Press projected
Virginians face a ballot measure asking them whether to approve redrawing their state’s congressional districts, which would give a big lift to Democrats’ goal of taking back the U.S. House
Republican officials are sounding the alarm ahead of November’s midterm elections, worried that the war in Iran and Democratic enthusiasm might mean a blowout
Rep. Eric Swalwell of California earned endorsements from top Democrats in his bid to become governor before sexual-misconduct allegations became public
The road to war last year featured rising profits at the weapons makers that supply the Pentagon with bombs, missiles and planes. Yet the war has been a different story.
John Phelan refused to accept his dismissal from the post of Navy secretary until he heard from President Trump
Former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has continued using a waterfront house on a military base in Washington, D.C.

The U.S. has burned through so many munitions in Iran that some administration officials assess that America couldn’t fully execute contingency plans to defend Taiwan from a Chinese invasion if it occurred in the near term, U.S. officials said
John Phelan has been fired as Navy secretary after months of simmering tension with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, according to U.S. officials
